bug_fix_report.reproduction: Replace the fixed 200 ms sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event; this deterministically reproduces the missing wake-up whenever the worker registers after the queue transition.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: Listener registration can occur after the one-shot completion transition, causing the wake-up to be lost.
confirming_evidence: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before worker completion, while passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.
With the listener-ordering fix, 500/500 stabilized runs passed.
With only the ordering fix removed, the stabilized test failed within 12 runs with the same missing wake-up.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Register the listener before the transition, leave production behavior otherwise unchanged, keep the condition-based test wait, and remove the fixed sleep.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Keep the condition-based test and hand it to pc-tdd; the stabilized check passed 500 consecutive runs with the fix.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The fixed 200 ms sleep permits the assertion to run before worker completion.
debug_journal.prediction: Failing runs check before worker completion.
debug_journal.experiment: Instrument event enqueue and worker completion timestamps without changing the wait.
debug_journal.result: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before completion; passing runs happened to complete inside 200 ms.

debug_journal.hypothesis: A condition-based wait will stabilize the race and reveal whether the completion event is eventually emitted.
debug_journal.prediction: The stabilized test will expose a missing wake-up when the worker registers after the queue transition.
debug_journal.experiment: Replace only the fixed sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event, leaving production code unchanged.
debug_journal.result: The test reproduced a missing wake-up whenever the worker registered after the queue transition.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Registering the listener before the transition prevents the one-shot wake-up from being lost.
debug_journal.prediction: The stabilized reproduction will pass with the ordering fix and fail again when only that fix is removed.
debug_journal.experiment: Change only worker registration ordering, run 500 stabilized checks, then remove only the ordering fix and rerun.
debug_journal.result: The fix passed 500/500 runs; removing only the fix returned the same missing-wake-up failure within 12 runs.
